reasoning: The document contains strong textual fidelity but multiple structural issues with hyperlinks. Specifically, there are displaced hyperlinks extracted as standalone elements rather than inline text, and missing hyperlink annotations (though some of the latter appears to be a source document limitation). With three major structural/hyperlink defects and a few minor formatting artifacts, the document falls squarely into the HUMAN_REVIEW category.

concerns.description: Link text pulled over but not hyperlinked
concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains clickable hyperlinks on page 4 and page 5 that were pulled into the output as plain text without corresponding link objects or markup. On page 4, the footnote link 'Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil)' is flattened. On page 5, the link 'Division's website.' under Question 19 is isolated as a plain text paragraph with no href or link object associated with it.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 4 footnote: 'Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil)' (hyperlink) and Page 5 under Question 19: 'Division's website.' (hyperlink).
concerns.output_quote: Page 4 JSON has '1 Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil) only one application needs to be submitted...' as a plain text paragraph. Page 5 JSON has a plain text paragraph 'Division's website.' Both lack link objects or anchor markup.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 3 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=3, examples=['https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/water-infrastructure/i-ne', 'https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/water-infrastructure/i-ne', 'https://www.epa.gov/G3/learn-about-green-streets']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: 1 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: (army.mil
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=1. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

The source PDF frequently uses underlining for emphasis in several answers, which is completely lost in the output text. As stated in the prompt, underline has no markdown equivalent, but its complete absence denotes a genuine loss of visual emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: constructing or repairing dams or other structures designed to retain waters within a stream channel is not eligible for funding.
concerns.output_quote: constructing or repairing dams or other structures designed to retain waters within a stream channel is not eligible for funding.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (1): LASII Stormwater Program Questions and Answers
LASII Program
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Source PDF contains 2 instance(s) of blue-styled text without hyperlink annotations. These appear to be intended as hyperlinks but were not annotated in the PDF (likely lost during Word-to-PDF export). This is a source document limitation, not a pipeline defect. Examples: page 4: "Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil)"; page 5: "Division's website."
concerns.analysis: orphaned_blue_text_count=2. Blue text without link annotations detected via PyMuPDF color extraction.

action_items: Fix displaced hyperlinks so they are inline within their respective paragraphs
Add href attributes to bare URLs and intended link text
Remove retained header/footer text and page numbers
Restore necessary visual emphasis where appropriate
